items.situation: checking if a service (post office, bank) is open
items.speaker: How late is the bank open on Saturdays?

options.A: Actually, I think they close at noon on Saturdays.
options.B: The bank across the street just got renovated.
options.C: I need to deposit a check sometime this week.
options.D: It's right next to the bookstore downtown.

items.explanation: 说话人问银行周六开到多晚，A项以"Actually"开头给出具体关门时间，直接回应问题。B项借用"bank"一词却谈翻新，跑题；C项谈自己要存支票，答非所问；D项回答的是"在哪"而非"开到几点"，问错类型。

items.situation: asking a neighbor about noise or shared space
items.speaker: What time does your band usually stop practicing?

options.A: My roommate plays guitar too, actually.
options.B: We usually wrap up by nine at night.
options.C: I've been practicing for a recital next month.
options.D: We practice in the basement studio.

items.explanation: 说话人直接问乐队通常几点结束排练，B项直接给出具体时间，回应最贴切。A项答非所问，谈起室友弹吉他；C项借用"practicing"一词却谈个人独奏会；D项回答的是"在哪排练"而非"几点结束"，问错类型。

items.situation: offering help to someone who seems stressed
items.speaker: Are you doing okay? You seem really stressed lately.

options.A: I heard the weather's supposed to be nice this weekend.
options.B: Stress management workshops are offered every Friday.
options.C: Actually, I could use someone to talk to.
options.D: No, I haven't seen that movie yet.

items.explanation: 对方关心地询问是否还好，C项以"Actually"开头坦诚回应，承认确实需要找人聊聊，最贴切。A项完全答非所问，谈天气；B项借用"stressed"一词却谈讲座活动；D项像是在回答一个关于电影的完全不同的问题。

items.situation: asking for directions to a nearby location
items.speaker: Where's the quickest way to get to the science building from here?

options.A: That building was renovated a few years ago.
options.B: I left my umbrella in my last class.
options.C: It usually takes about ten minutes to walk there.
options.D: I'm headed that way now, so just follow me.

items.explanation: 对方问最快去理科楼的路怎么走，D项没有直接描述路线，而是提议直接带路，从根本上解决了对方的需求，属于"context_shift"型正确答案。A项借用"building"一词却谈翻新历史；B项完全答非所问；C项回答的是"要花多久"而非"怎么走"，问错类型。

items.situation: friend sharing news or personal update
items.speaker: Why do you seem so excited today?

options.A: I just got accepted into the study abroad program.
options.B: The cafeteria added a new sandwich to the menu.
options.C: Excitement can sometimes make it hard to focus.
options.D: It happened earlier this morning, actually.

items.explanation: 对方问为什么今天这么兴奋，A项直接说明了兴奋的原因（拿到留学项目录取），回应最贴切。B项完全答非所问，谈餐厅菜单；C项借用"excited"一词却泛泛而谈情绪本身；D项回答的是"什么时候发生的"而非"为什么"，问错类型。

items.situation: student asking about dorm maintenance issue
items.speaker: Do you know if maintenance fixed the heater in our room yet?

options.A: Heaters in older buildings tend to break down often.
options.B: Actually, someone's supposed to come by this afternoon.
options.C: My roommate switched to a different meal plan.
options.D: The maintenance office is on the ground floor.

items.explanation: 对方问维修人员是否已经修好暖气，B项以"Actually"开头说明维修人员下午会来，间接但贴切地回应了问题。A项借用"heaters"一词却泛泛而谈老楼通病；C项完全答非所问；D项回答的是"维修办公室在哪"而非"修好了没"，问错类型。

items.situation: acknowledging someone's unavailability or schedule conflict
items.speaker: I won't be able to make it to the meeting tonight.

options.A: The meeting room was recently repainted.
options.B: I forgot to charge my phone this morning.
options.C: No worries, I'll send you the notes afterward.
options.D: Yes, it starts at seven o'clock.

items.explanation: 对方说自己今晚开不了会，C项以"No worries"开头，体贴地表示会后补发笔记，回应最贴切。A项借用"meeting"一词却谈房间装修；B项完全答非所问；D项像是在回答一个关于几点开始的问题，而非回应对方缺席这件事。

items.situation: expressing concern about a friend's situation
items.speaker: I'm worried about my roommate, she's barely eating anything these days.

options.A: The dining hall changed its dinner menu recently.
options.B: I've been meaning to try that new restaurant downtown.
options.C: She'll probably be back to campus by Friday.
options.D: Maybe we should both check in on her together.

items.explanation: 说话人表达了对室友的担忧，D项没有停留在字面的"吃饭"话题上，而是提议一起去关心室友，从根本上回应了这份担忧，属于"context_shift"型正确答案。A项借用"eating"一词却谈餐厅菜单；B项完全答非所问；C项像是在回答一个关于室友何时返校的完全不同的问题。
